steps.title: Identify the relationship and radius
steps.content: To find mass, we need: Mass = Density × Volume. For a sphere, we'll use its volume formula. Given diameter = 31.000 cm, the radius is $r = \frac{31}{2} = 15.5$ cm. We have density = 2.6 g/cm³ and π = 3.14159.

steps.title: Calculate sphere volume
steps.content:
Compute the radius cubed: $(15.5)^3 = 15.5 \times 15.5 \times 15.5 = 240.25 \times 15.5 = 3{,}723.875$ cm³

Then apply the formula: $V = \frac{4}{3} \times 3.14159 \times 3{,}723.875 \approx 15{,}598.52$ cm³
